content.problemStatement:
There is a magician fighting against NNN cities. City iii has an army with a strength of AiA_iAi​.

Let KKK denote the city where the magician is currently at.

Initially, K=1K = 1K=1.

Every second, the magician can do **exactly one** of the following:


If K<NK \lt NK<N, move to the (K+1)(K+1)(K+1)-th city and hence increase KKK by 111; **or**
Choose an index i≤Ki \leq Ki≤K and set AiA_iAi​ to 000, hence destroying the iii-th city's army.

Note that this move does not change KKK.
After the magician has made his move, every city will attack the magician, and he will take damage equal to the current sum of all the armies' strengths, that is, (A1+A2+…+AN)\left(A_1 + A_2 + \ldots + A_N\right)(A1​+A2​+…+AN​).

If the magician chooses his moves optimally, find the minimum possible total damage he will take before every army is destroyed.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN — the number of cities.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible total damage the magician must take.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 500050005000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Consider the following sequence of spells:

Choose i=1i=1i=1 and set Ai=0A_i = 0Ai​=0.
The magician will now take 000 damage, and the process since every army has been destroyed.

Test case 222: Consider the following sequence of spells:

The magician will move to city 222, so K=2K = 2K=2. The array remains A=[1,3]A = [1, 3]A=[1,3], so he will take 1+3=41+3 = 41+3=4 damage.
Choose i=2i=2i=2 and set A2=0A_2 = 0A2​=0. The array is now [1,0][1, 0][1,0], so he will take 1+0=11+0 = 11+0=1 damage.
Choose i=1i=1i=1 and set A1=0A_1 = 0A1​=0. The array is now [0,0][0, 0][0,0], so he will take 0+0=00+0 = 00+0=0 damage.
All armies have been destroyed, so the process ends.

The total damage taken is 4+1=54+1 = 54+1=5. It can be shown that this is the minimum possible.
